def test_jj_status_clean():
        """After restore, the working-copy diff must be empty (no modified files)."""
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["jj", "diff", "--no-pager", "--summary"],
            cwd=REPO_DIR,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
        )
        assert result.returncode == 0, f"jj diff failed: {result.stderr}"
>       assert result.stdout.strip() == "", (
            f"Expected empty diff (clean working copy) but got:\n{result.stdout}"
        )
E       AssertionError: Expected empty diff (clean working copy) but got:
E         M config/settings.toml
E         A src/metrics.py
E         
E       assert 'M config/set...rc/metrics.py' == ''
E         
E         + M config/settings.toml
E         + A src/metrics.py

tests.trace:
def test_operation_log_contains_restore():
        """The jj operation log must contain a restore operation."""
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["jj", "op", "log", "--no-pager", "--no-graph", "--limit", "10",
             "-T", "description ++ '\\n'"],
            cwd=REPO_DIR,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
        )
        assert result.returncode == 0, f"jj op log failed: {result.stderr}"
>       assert "restore" in result.stdout, (
            f"Expected 'restore' operation in op log but not found.\nOutput:\n{result.stdout}"
        )
E       AssertionError: Expected 'restore' operation in op log but not found.
E         Output:
E         snapshot working copy\ndescribe commit fa8ff7a341c5b1925e3989f4681630b0991049fd\nsnapshot working copy\nnew empty commit\ndescribe commit 9d3137daeac588abb5509dff8464a9952f58c860\nsnapshot working copy\nnew empty commit\ndescribe commit 77650f8e9b16ddaec04cf233ef5d05e71bb9b067\nsnapshot working copy\nadd workspace 'default'\n
E       assert 'restore' in "snapshot working copy\\ndescribe commit fa8ff7a341c5b1925e3989f4681630b0991049fd\\nsnapshot working copy\\nnew empty ...commit\\ndescribe commit 77650f8e9b16ddaec04cf233ef5d05e71bb9b067\\nsnapshot working copy\\nadd workspace 'default'\\n"
E        +  where "snapshot working copy\\ndescribe commit fa8ff7a341c5b1925e3989f4681630b0991049fd\\nsnapshot working copy\\nnew empty ...commit\\ndescribe commit 77650f8e9b16ddaec04cf233ef5d05e71bb9b067\\nsnapshot working copy\\nadd workspace 'default'\\n" = CompletedProcess(args=['jj', 'op', 'log', '--no-pager', '--no-graph', '--limit', '10', '-T', "description ++ '\\n'"], ...cribe commit 77650f8e9b16ddaec04cf233ef5d05e71bb9b067\\nsnapshot working copy\\nadd workspace 'default'\\n", stderr='').stdout

tests.name: test_final_state.py::test_parent_commit_description_still_wip
tests.trace:
def test_parent_commit_description_still_wip():
        """The parent of the working-copy commit must still be 'wip: integrate metrics module'."""
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["jj", "log", "--no-pager", "--no-graph", "-T", "description ++ '\\n'",
             "-r", "@-"],
            cwd=REPO_DIR,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
        )
        assert result.returncode == 0, f"jj log failed: {result.stderr}"
>       assert "wip: integrate metrics module" in result.stdout, (
            f"Expected parent commit 'wip: integrate metrics module' but got:\n{result.stdout}"
        )
E       AssertionError: Expected parent commit 'wip: integrate metrics module' but got:
E         feat: add config module
E         \n
E       assert 'wip: integrate metrics module' in 'feat: add config module\n\\n'
E        +  where 'feat: add config module\n\\n' = CompletedProcess(args=['jj', 'log', '--no-pager', '--no-graph', '-T', "description ++ '\\n'", '-r', '@-'], returncode=0, stdout='feat: add config module\n\\n', stderr='').stdout

tests.name: test_final_state.py::test_jj_status_output
tests.trace:
def test_jj_status_output():
        """jj status must report a clean working copy."""
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["jj", "status", "--no-pager"],
            cwd=REPO_DIR,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
        )
        assert result.returncode == 0, f"jj status failed: {result.stderr}"
        # A clean jj working copy shows no modified/added/removed files in the status output
        # The output should not list any M/A/D file entries
        lines = [l.strip() for l in result.stdout.splitlines() if l.strip()]
        file_change_lines = [l for l in lines if l and l[0] in ("M", "A", "D") and len(l) > 2]
>       assert file_change_lines == [], (
            f"Unexpected file changes in 'jj status':\n{chr(10).join(file_change_lines)}"
        )
E       AssertionError: Unexpected file changes in 'jj status':
E         M config/settings.toml
E         A src/metrics.py
E       assert ['M config/se...c/metrics.py'] == []
E         
E         Left contains 2 more items, first extra item: 'M config/settings.toml'
E         Use -v to get more diff
